Now we're writing up what we found into the evidence packet…

⚠️ The chart is missing something for question 1: The March visit recorded BSA and IGA but never repeated the PASI score that was tracked at every other visit.

⚠️ The chart is missing something for question 2: The June dermatology visit never re-documented the nail findings that nursing recorded in May, so nail progression after May cannot be tracked from the notes.

⚠️ The chart is missing something for question 4: The June visit deferred the adherence and pharmacy-fill review to a separate document that is not in the chart, so there is no record showing whether the patient actually took the current treatment as prescribed.

⚠️ The chart is missing something for question 5: None of the visits document a check for the common causes of a flare — missed medication doses, a recent infection, a steroid taper, or a seasonal pattern — and the separately filed adherence review is missing from the chart.
Our evidence packet is ready — handing it to Agent B for review.
Round 1: adversarial review
We're checking Agent A's evidence against the rubric. We never see the chart ourselves — only what A quoted, plus outside records like the pharmacy's if we need them.
The severity scores, symptom trajectory, and DLQI trend all check out — three PAS
I values, corroborating photographs, and a sustained 7-plus point DLQI rise are documented with dated
quotes. But the whole worsening determination rests on an assumption nobody has verified: the adherence review was filed separately and is missing, and no confounder

screening exists anywhere in the chart. The packet honestly marks both items insufficient, yet the worsening claim still stands on the severity criterion while adherence is a live open question. We're pulling
the pharmacy fill records to check whether the patient actually had drug coverage over the deterioration interval.

The severity scores, symptom trajectory, and DLQI trend all check out — three PASI values, corroborating photographs, and a sustained 7-plus point DLQI rise are documented with dated quotes. But the whole worsening determination rests on an assumption nobody has verified: the adherence review was filed separately and is missing, and no confounder screening exists anywhere in the chart. The packet honestly marks both items insufficient, yet the worsening claim still stands on the severity criterion while adherence is a live open question. We're pulling the pharmacy fill records to check whether the patient actually had drug coverage over the deterioration interval.
Acting as the nurse, we called up the pharmacy's dispense records to see what was actually filled — 5 record(s) on file.
The pharmacy feed settles the open question, and not in the packet's favor. The adal
imumab fills came every four weeks like clockwork — January 19, February 16, March 16 — and then stopped. Last fill 2026-03-16 with a 28-day supply means drug coverage ended 2026-04-13.

Every piece of worsening evidence in the packet (PASI 14.2 on 2026-05-10, PASI 16.8 on 2026-06-29, the new nail and scalp findings, the DLQI jump) postdates that lapse. The scores and photos themselves hold up, but the attribution to
disease activity does not: the entire deterioration interval overlaps an unexcluded treatment gap. The packet already concedes criterion 4 honestly, so the challenge bel

ongs on the confounder criterion, where the unknown has now become a concrete, documented non-adherence confounder.

MedicationDispense", "resource_id": "MD-003", "detail": "prior adalimumab dispense 2026-02-16, 28-day supply — fills had been on a regular 4-week cadence (with MD-002 on 2026-01-19) until they
stopped after 2026-03-16"}]}]

The pharmacy feed settles the open question, and not in the packet's favor. The adalimumab fills came every four weeks like clockwork — January 19, February 16, March 16 — and then stopped. Last fill 2026-03-16 with a 28-day supply means drug coverage ended 2026-04-13. Every piece of worsening evidence in the packet (PASI 14.2 on 2026-05-10, PASI 16.8 on 2026-06-29, the new nail and scalp findings, the DLQI jump) postdates that lapse. The scores and photos themselves hold up, but the attribution to disease activity does not: the entire deterioration interval overlaps an unexcluded treatment gap. The packet already concedes criterion 4 honestly, so the challenge belongs on the confounder …

On question 5: The pharmacy records show the last adalimumab fill was March 16 with a 28-day supply, so the drug ran out April 13 and was never refilled. Every measurement showing the patient getting worse comes after that date, so we can't tell whether this is the disease progressing or simply the medication stopping.

data.rubric_quote: A confounder is 'addressed' only if the chart either excludes it or establishes that worsening preceded it.
data.what_would_satisfy: Evidence that the severity rise persisted during a period of confirmed adherence, or a concession that the progression cannot be separated from the treatment gap.

external_evidence.detail: last adalimumab dispense 2026-03-16, 28-day supply; coverage ended 2026-04-13; no subsequent adalimumab fill before the 2026-05-10 and 2026-06-29 visits documenting worsening
prior adalimumab dispense 2026-02-16, 28-day supply — fills had been on a regular 4-week cadence (with MD-002 on 2026-01-19) until they stopped after 2026-03-16

We concede question 5 — the chart can't answer B's objection, so we're marking it 'not enough documentation'.
Round 2: adversarial review
We're checking Agent A's evidence against the rubric. We never see the chart ourselves — only what A quoted, plus outside records like the pharmacy's if we need them.
The severity scores, DLQI values, nail and sc
alp findings are all documented with dated quotes across multiple visits, and the photographs corroborate the trend — criteria 1 through 3
hold up as measured trends. Critically, the packet does not overreach: criteria 1 and 2

explicitly note they document the trend only and defer attribution to criterion 5. Criterion 5 is conceded as unresolved, citing the adalimumab dispense gap starting 2026-04-13 that precedes every measurement of the latest worsening, and it states exactly what would settle it
— dated evidence the deterioration began before the gap, or reassessment after confirmed adherence.

Criterion 4 likewise concedes the adherent trial can't be verified. These concessions match what the dispense record shows, so there is nothing left to challenge and no need to re-pull the pharmacy feed.

[]

The severity scores, DLQI values, nail and scalp findings are all documented with dated quotes across multiple visits, and the photographs corroborate the trend — criteria 1 through 3 hold up as measured trends. Critically, the packet does not overreach: criteria 1 and 2 explicitly note they document the trend only and defer attribution to criterion 5. Criterion 5 is conceded as unresolved, citing the adalimumab dispense gap starting 2026-04-13 that precedes every measurement of the latest worsening, and it states exactly what would settle it — dated evidence the deterioration began before the gap, or reassessment after confirmed adherence. Criterion 4 likewise concedes the adherent trial …
We have no objections — the evidence holds up under the rubric.
INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE
We're writing a plain-language text message to the patient about what happens next…

Hi, we looked over your recent visits and your psoriasis does look more active. But it seems some doses of your adalimumab were missed, so it's hard to tell what's causing the flare. Please restart it as prescribed, and reply to let us know about when your skin started getting worse. If you need help getting the medicine, just tell us. — your dermatology care team
